compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body:
Root cause + fix implemented on branch fix/optionality-failure-lazy-import.

FIX (src/rebar/llm/failure.py): removed the module-scope `import httpx` and
`from pydantic_ai.exceptions import (...)`. Those names are now imported LAZILY
inside `_map()` and `_base_diagnostic()` (the two functions doing runtime
isinstance checks), each guarded by `try/except ImportError` so classification
degrades to NEEDS_INVESTIGATION (non-retryable) — never ModuleNotFoundError —
when the [agents] stack is absent (e.g. runner.preflight classifying a
missing-extra LLMConfigError). Annotations on the two private HTTP helpers relaxed
to `Any` (module-scope heavy-type annotations, incl. under `if TYPE_CHECKING:`,
are ALSO forbidden by the AST guard, which recurses into If bodies).

AST-GUARD GAP (tests/unit/test_core_optionality.py): `_HEAVY` was a stale
LangChain-era list (langchain/langgraph/anthropic/inspect_ai/opentelemetry/...).
Added `pydantic_ai` and `httpx` — the actual runtime heavy deps after the
pydantic-ai cutover (story d6d1). Verified RED-before / GREEN-after: with the
addition, the guard flags exactly failure.py (sole offender) pre-fix and passes
post-fix. failure.py is the ONLY module-scope offender in the core tree (all of
runner.py's pydantic_ai/httpx imports are already function-local).

VERIFICATION so far (repo .venv, local build):
- make lint: pass; make typecheck: pass (297 files).
- tests/unit/test_core_optionality.py: 2 passed (guard now catches this class).
- tests/unit/test_llm_failure_matrix.py: 35 passed (classification unchanged w/ extras).
- Lean-path proof: with httpx+pydantic_ai import-blocked, `import rebar.llm.failure`
  succeeds and classify -> NEEDS_INVESTIGATION (non-retryable), no raise.
- optionality + llm-optionality subset: 51 passed, 11 skipped (clean-lane-only).
- Full `make test`: running.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body:
DECISION — Gerrit Verified path (deliverable #4): the module-scope-heavy-import
guard is ALREADY in the Verified path; the residual clean-wheel/packaging jobs are
justified to stay post-merge.

Findings:
- gerrit-verify.yaml (the sole `Verified` voter) runs the SAME checks as test.yml
  against the exact patchset, installing `.[dev]` -> `[agents]` (full heavy stack).
- optionality.yml runs on `push`/`pull_request` only — i.e. GitHub branch/PR CI. It
  casts NO Gerrit vote. Since code lands via Gerrit (not GitHub PRs), its
  clean-core-wheel job only ran POST-merge on `main`, which is why the regression
  reached `main` visibly-red instead of being blocked.
- BUT: the static AST guard
  `tests/unit/test_core_optionality.py::test_no_core_module_imports_heavy_stack_at_module_scope`
  is a normal unit test collected by `pytest -m "not integration and not external"`
  == `make test` == the gerrit-verify default suite. Confirmed it is selected under
  that marker filter. So it DOES run in the Verified gate on every patchset.

Consequence: fixing `_HEAVY` (this ticket) extends the Verified gate to block THIS
regression class (module-scope heavy imports) pre-merge, deterministically and for
free — no workflow rewiring. This is the exact mechanism of the incident, so the gap
is genuinely closed in the pre-merge gate.

Why NOT fold the full optionality workflow (clean-core-wheel + per-extra + union
jobs) into gerrit-verify:
1. Disproportionate cost. Those jobs need a fundamentally different install matrix
   (build wheel -> N fresh no-extras venvs -> per-extra installs -> union resolve).
   They cannot reuse build-and-test's already-`[dev]` environment, so they'd be
   several NEW parallel jobs on EVERY per-patchset Verified vote (and under FF-only
   submit, re-run on every rebase). Verified latency is already the throughput
   bottleneck for landing.
2. The high-value, high-frequency failure mode (module-scope heavy import) is now
   covered in-Verified by the cheap in-process AST test. The clean-wheel job's unique
   residual coverage is (a) dynamic/transitive import leaks the AST can't see and
   (b) packaging/extras-resolution regressions — both lower-frequency, and (b) is
   better gated at release than per-change.
3. The clean-wheel jobs still run on every push to main (post-merge) and on the
   GitHub PR mirror, so the safety net remains — it is just not a submit-blocker.

Net: partial add (the guard that matters, already wired) + justified deferral of the
heavyweight packaging jobs. If the team prefers belt-and-suspenders, the follow-up
would be to extract optionality's jobs into a reusable workflow called from both
optionality.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml — filed as a separate optionality ticket if desired.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body:
Pushed for review: Gerrit change 529 — https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/529
(commit 57dde4b, on top of origin/main 2d1524ad8).

Full `make test` on the repo .venv: 5321 passed, 14 skipped, 100 deselected, and
exactly 1 FAILED — tests/unit/test_resolve_symbol_tool.py::
test_resolve_symbol_confirms_third_party_module. That failure is PRE-EXISTING and
environmental, NOT from this change: it fails identically with my diff stashed on
pristine origin/main. Cause: this machine's `.venv` lives INSIDE the repo tree
(origin=/Users/.../rebar/.venv/.../yaml/__init__.py) on Python 3.14, so the symbol
resolver classifies `yaml` as "repo-local" instead of "third-party/stdlib". CI runs
3.12 with the venv outside the working dir, so it resolves correctly there. It does
not touch resolve_symbol and is unrelated to failure.py/optionality.

Close gate: NOT closing until Gerrit Verified=+1 lands on this patchset (CI green on
the exact tree), per project policy.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: REOPENING — premature close. My import fix (Gerrit 529, merged) correctly fixed the module-scope import, but the optionality clean-core-wheel job on the merged main tip (57dde4b16) STILL FAILS at a LATER step (degradation-path tests) that was previously MASKED behind the import error. Two failures in tests/interfaces/store/test_llm_optionality.py: (1) line 163 'review_code is fail-safe (off by default) -> exit 0' got exit 1; (2) test_mcp_operations_error_cleanly_when_gated_on_but_extra_absent 'DID NOT RAISE'. These SKIP when the agents extra is installed, so my local (extras-present) run never exercised them. Root lesson: the Verified vote (gerrit-verify) does NOT run the clean-wheel job, so Verified+1 did not prove optionality green. Investigating whether these are my regression or pre-existing-unmasked breakage.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body:
DIAGNOSIS of the 2 unmasked clean-wheel failures (both pre-existing, from story
authorial-hated-blackbear; hidden by the import bug until Gerrit 529 let the
degradation-path pytest step run):

1. CLI review-code exit code (test_cli_operation_degrades_without_extra[review_code]):
   The library review_code() correctly returns the inert disabled result
   {findings:[], runner:"code-review-disabled", summary:"...disabled..."} with no raise
   — my failure.py fix is fine. But the CLI `rebar review-code` maps that result to
   exit 1 via _disposition_exit_code (no `verdict`, no `coverage.retryable` -> generic
   non-PASS -> 1). For an off-by-default fail-safe capability it must be exit 0.
   FIX: _review_code returns 0 for the disabled code-review result.

2. verify_completion "DID NOT RAISE" (test_mcp_operations_error_cleanly_when_gated_on_
   but_extra_absent): verify_completion intentionally catches LLMError and returns
   _structured_llm_failure(exc) = {error:"...agents...", resolution_class:
   NEEDS_INVESTIGATION, retryable:False, diagnostic:{...}} — a structured degrade, NOT
   a raise. THREE MCP tools do this (review_code, verify_completion, review_plan);
   review_ticket/scan_spec raise. The test's must-raise `forced` set exempts review_code
   (comment) but forgot verify_completion. It is a STALE test, not a code bug (the close
   gate consumes this structured verdict and fail-closes correctly —
   test_gate_missing_llm_fails_closed passes).
   FIX: exempt verify_completion from the must-raise set and assert its structured-degrade
   contract (error surfaces 'agents', resolution_class present, not silent/empty).

Both will be verified in a true no-extras clean venv (where these tests actually run;
they SKIP when the agents extra is present, which is why my first local run missed them).
Landing as a second change stacked on 529.

compiled_state.description:
## Summary

The `optionality` GitHub Actions workflow fails on every push to `main` (job
"clean core wheel (no extras → heavy deps absent)"). `src/rebar/llm/failure.py`
imports `httpx` and `pydantic_ai.exceptions` at **module scope**, so importing
the module in a no-extras (core-only) install raises
`ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'pydantic_ai'` — breaking the lean-runtime
optionality contract (extras may only ADD deps; core must import with none).

It reached `main` because the Gerrit `Verified` gate (gerrit-verify.yaml) installs
`.[dev]` -> `[agents]` (heavy stack present), so the module-scope import succeeds
there; and the static AST guard that SHOULD catch it
(`tests/unit/test_core_optionality.py::test_no_core_module_imports_heavy_stack_at_module_scope`,
which DOES run inside `make test` = the Verified suite) has a stale `_HEAVY` list
from the LangChain era — it is missing `pydantic_ai` and `httpx`, the actual
runtime heavy deps after the pydantic-ai cutover.

## Reproduction Steps

1. Build the core wheel with no extras, install into a clean venv
   (`python -m build --wheel && pip install dist/*.whl`).
2. `python -c "import rebar.llm.failure"`.

- Expected: import succeeds (core stays lean; heavy imports are lazy).
- Actual: `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'pydantic_ai'` at
  `src/rebar/llm/failure.py:27`; optionality clean-core-wheel job reports
  `8 failed, 11 passed`.

## Root Cause

- `failure.py` module-scope `import httpx` + `from pydantic_ai.exceptions import (...)`.
- `_HEAVY` in `tests/unit/test_core_optionality.py` omits `pydantic_ai`/`httpx`,
  so the in-Verified static guard never flagged the offender.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `src/rebar/llm/failure.py` performs NO module-scope import of `httpx` or
      `pydantic_ai`; those imports are lazy (function-local), matching the
      established pattern in `runner.py`. Type annotations remain valid under
      `from __future__ import annotations`.
- [ ] `classify_llm_failure(...)` still classifies correctly WITH the agents
      extra present, and degrades safely (returns NEEDS_INVESTIGATION, never
      raises ModuleNotFoundError) when the heavy stack is ABSENT.
- [ ] `_HEAVY` in `tests/unit/test_core_optionality.py` includes `pydantic_ai`
      and `httpx`, so the static module-scope guard catches this regression class.
- [ ] The AST guard fails on the unfixed code (RED) and passes after the fix (GREEN).
- [ ] The optionality workflow clean-core-wheel job passes (module imports under
      a no-extras install).
- [ ] Decision recorded on the Gerrit Verified path: the module-scope-heavy-import
      guard is confirmed to run inside the Verified suite via the AST test; the
      residual clean-wheel/packaging jobs are justified as post-merge (or wired in).
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass locally.

file_impact.reason: lazy heavy imports (httpx/pydantic_ai) at call boundary

compiled_state.title: optionality CI red on main: src/rebar/llm/failure.py imports pydantic_ai/httpx at module scope
